lifecycle_conformance_tests.description: Validates canonical execute -> read_output -> terminate sequencing and post-termination drain-to-EOF behavior.
Validates read_output during session_starting remains observational and terminate transitions deterministically to termination.
Validates post-termination read_output returns terminal-state semantics and execute requires a newly allocated session identity.

race_condition_tests.description: Validates concurrent terminate/read_output overlap on one session resolves deterministically without duplicate mutation.
Validates repeated terminate calls under race pressure emit one effective kill signal with idempotent replay outcomes.
Validates duplicate read_output requests at identical cursor while terminate is in-flight keep cursor stability and deterministic EOF progression.

multi_session_stress_tests.description: Validates concurrent session execution and reads preserve per-session isolation boundaries under parallel load.
Validates per-session buffer ceilings and truncation semantics under sustained output pressure without cross-session corruption.
Validates terminating a subset of sessions does not clear locks, buffers, or environment state of still-active sessions.

container_bridge_fault_tests.description: Validates container_bridge preflight alias-resolution failure follows ordered fallback before session allocation and emits deterministic diagnostics.
Validates bridge connect-timeout preflight behavior rejects without session allocation when fallback candidates are exhausted.
Validates post-allocation bridge loss triggers per-session bounded recovery with cross-session isolation preserved.

stages.description: Run extension-local conformance checks before Podman resilience execution.
Run Podman bridge resilience lane after local conformance passes.

pass_when_all: all freshness checks satisfy thresholds within post_recovery_window_ms
fail_when_any: any freshness check exceeds configured threshold
route/session state remains stale beyond post_recovery_window_ms

cases.description: Resume handshake succeeds with valid token, lease, and replay cursor under nominal latency.
Resume handshake remains valid when response latency is delayed but below timeout ceiling.
Duplicate reconnect attempts within suppression window return existing result and do not duplicate subscription/replay emit.
Reconnect must rehydrate route/session context after connectivity restore before lane success is emitted.
Stale or expired token is denied and requires full resync path.

scenarios.description: Dashboard API endpoint is forced unavailable and then restored to validate outage detection and reconnect recovery.
WebSocket transport path is interrupted to validate reconnect sequencing and readiness-gated restore behavior.
Backend service restart and recovery path verifies stable reconnection and post-restart state restoration.
Container bridge preflight is forced to fail to validate ordered fallback, no-session-allocation hard-fail path, and deterministic diagnostics.
Container bridge runtime disconnect is injected after allocation to validate bounded per-session recovery and required diagnostics emission.

container_boundary_failure_taxonomy.description: Four-layer failure taxonomy for Podman container boundary faults. All boundary faults must be classified into one layer before recovery actions are applied.

runtime.description: Faults originating from the container execution environment (process, OOM, resource).

fault_classes.trigger: container fails to reach healthy within start_period + interval * retries
container restarts exceed restart_loop_threshold within restart_loop_window_ms

network.description: Faults originating from Podman bridge networking, DNS resolution, or port publishing.

fault_classes.trigger: service loses reachability within integration_net while process is running
internal compose DNS fails to resolve service hostname
published port not reachable from host
container-bridge TCP channel loses connectivity after session allocation

storage.description: Faults originating from volume, bind-mount, or named-volume integrity issues.

fault_classes.trigger: named volume or bind-mount fails to attach at container start
run-scoped data tree is corrupt or missing required subdirectories
container killed without graceful shutdown; named volume partially written
service writes outside declared mount paths

application.description: Faults originating from service-level logic, misconfiguration, or dependency readiness violations.

fault_classes.trigger: service running and reachable but health endpoint returns status=unhealthy
dependent service starts traffic before upstream readiness gate passes
service uses env vars outside declared environment/env_file contract
application-level circuit breaker opens after consecutive failures

isolation_policy.description: Single-service fault containment rules. A fault in one compose service MUST NOT cascade to restart services outside the fault's declared failure domain.

containment_scope_map.child-local: Restart limited to the faulted service container only. No peer or dependent services are restarted.
containment_scope_map.dependency-group: Restart limited to the faulted service and its declared compose depends_on chain. Unrelated services must remain healthy.
containment_scope_map.global: Restart of the full stack. FORBIDDEN under normal operating conditions. Requires explicit operator action and must emit the global_restart_operator_triggered reason code.

cascade_gate.description: If service X faults, services not in X's dependency chain must not restart. Any restart outside scope emits an isolation violation event.

dependency_restart_choreography.description: Ordered restart and recovery choreography for compose service dependency chains after a container fault.

storage_integrity_policy.description: Storage integrity verification protocol for named volumes after abrupt container termination.

fail.description: Corrupt volume must not be auto-restarted. Requires operator action or backup restore to prevent data loss escalation.

resource_boundary_policy.description: Resource-boundary safeguard rules and degradation behavior under CPU, memory, and IO pressure.

podman_network_fault_verification_matrix.description: Podman Compose network fault verification tests covering bridge isolation, DNS lookup failure, and delayed route restoration.

scenarios.description: Simulate bridge network isolation between compose services to verify dependency-group scoped recovery and reconnect gate re-evaluation.
Force internal compose DNS resolution failure for a service hostname and verify that readiness gate blocks reconnect until DNS is restored.
Inject a delayed route restoration after bridge network loss to verify reconnect latency remains within recovery SLO thresholds.

storage_volume_fault_verification_matrix.description: Storage and volume fault verification tests covering permission denial, mount loss, and delayed persistence availability.

scenarios.description: Deny write permission to the named volume mount for project-memory service to verify that integrity gate blocks restart and prevents data corruption.
Detach the named volume from project-memory mid-session and verify that integrity check gate is enforced before any restart is attempted.
Delay volume availability on service restart to verify that pre-start gate waits for storage readiness before admitting service traffic.

startup_order_readiness_fault_verificati.description: Startup-order and readiness fault tests covering dependency timing races, restart storms, and readiness gate timeouts under slow-start conditions.

scenarios.description: Force project-memory to start before its database volume is fully initialized to verify readiness gate blocks traffic until all depends_on conditions are satisfied.
Simulate repeated rapid restart of project-memory to verify reconnect storm policy suppresses excess reconnect attempts and enforces cooldown.
Slow-start project-memory to verify that readiness gate times out gracefully, emits the correct reason code, and does not admit traffic until health check passes.

health_validation_thresholds.description: Health validation thresholds and pass/fail criteria for recovery latency and stability across all Phase 3 Podman verification lanes.
